(a) GNA in the present case was granted to the Petitioner under Regulation 17.1(iii) of
the GNA Regulations, 2022.

While Regulation 18 of the Connectivity
Regulations, 2009 was specifically related to payment of relinquishment charges
with respect to stranded capacity, there is no such intention, express or otherwise,
prescribed in the proviso to Regulation 25.1 of the GNA Regulations, 2022.

(1) A long-term customer may Provided further that f...

obligations.obligation:
The Petitioner, while
interpreting the proviso to Regulation 25.1 of the GNA Regulations, 2022, to include
the concept of stranded capacity into it, is adding words to the Regulation that are
impermissible and contrary to the settled principle of interpretation.

applicable in the present case.

coordinated and economical system of inter-state transmission lines.

obligations.obligation:

the word ‘economical’ is to ensure that the cost of availing open access by the
DICs is commensurate with the transmission capacity/ services availed of by
DICs. In other words, the use of open access by the DICs should not become
onerous, which may include scenarios where the cost of an ...

become effective or not; this principle is the underlying intent while levying

an economical manner so that relinquishment of capacity by a GNA grantee
should not impact the cost of availing open access by the other DICs. CTUIL’s
contention that reliance on Para 16.2.1 of the SOR is inapplicab...

However, the reference to
the SOR is made with respect to the principle that relinquishment of GNA
quantum should not lead to an increase in the share of charges for other DICs,
which is the underlying intent while levying relinquishment charges under
Regulation 25.

is also captured under SOR to Regulation 25.1 of the GNA Regulations, 2022.

CENTRAL ELECTRICITY REGULATORY COMMISSION
NEW DELHI
Petition No. 451/MP/2025 along with IA 36/2025
Coram:
Shri Jishnu Barua, Chairperson
Shri Ramesh Babu V., Member
Shri Harish Dudani, Member
Shri Ravinder Singh Dhillon, Member
Date of Order: 14.02.2026
In the matter of:
Petition under Section 79, including 79 (1)(c), 79 (1)(f) and 79 (1)(k) of the Electricity
Act, 2003, seeking continuation of its GNA/ Connectivity of 700 MW. The Petitioner is
also seeking consequent directions upon the Respondent/ CTUIL to consider
Connectivity Bank Guarantees already furnished by the Petitioner with respect to its
GNA applications dated 30.05.2024 to 22.06.2024 as sufficient for the purpose of such
continuation, with no requirement to submit fresh Connectivity Bank Guarantees.
